macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Decline Sharply on Tariff Uncertainty
items.summary: Major US equity indices fell on Monday as President Trump implemented 15% tariffs under Section 122, creating renewed uncertainty over trade policy. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 dropped 1.2%, while the Dow fell 800 points, with losses led by American Express (-4.99%), Nike (-4.01%), and JPMorgan (-2.38%).

items.title: Tech Giants and Payment Operators Hit by AI Displacement Concerns
items.summary: Software and payment sector stocks took fresh losses amid AI displacement fears, with Oracle and Palantir dropping 4% and American Express tanking 7%. Nvidia was slightly higher ahead of its earnings announcement this week.

items.title: Capital Economics Warns of Stock Market Bubble Bursting in 2027
items.summary: Capital Economics forecasts a stock market bubble will burst in 2027, noting that the current rotation toward small-cap, value, and defensive stocks mirrors patterns from the late dot-com era. The shift has gathered momentum through early 2026 despite elevated overall market levels.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Whales Face $26 Billion in Unrealized Losses
items.summary: Short-term Bitcoin whales are holding about $26 billion in unrealized losses, marking one of the highest levels this year. This reflects significant pressure on large holders amid recent market conditions.

items.title: Bitdeer Liquidates All Bitcoin Holdings for AI Expansion
items.summary: Bitdeer sold 943.1 BTC from reserves and 189.8 BTC of newly mined coins, bringing its balance to zero. Proceeds are being redirected toward data center expansion and AI cloud infrastructure, highlighting miners' shift to high-performance compute.

items.title: Wall Street Banks Experiment with Canton Blockchain Over Ethereum
items.summary: Major banks like JPMorgan Chase and Visa are testing Canton, a privacy-focused blockchain not built on Ethereum, amid debates on blockchain privacy for traditional finance. Ethereum's ZKsync is seen as a competing solution by the crypto community.

items.title: Dragonfly Raises $650 Million Fund Amid VC 'Extinction' Prediction
items.summary: Venture firm Dragonfly, with investments in Polymarket and Ethena, closed a $650 million fourth fund. One partner forecasts a 'mass extinction event' in the VC ecosystem.

items.title: BlockFills Halts Deposits and Withdrawals After $75 Million Loss
items.summary: Susquehanna-backed crypto lender BlockFills suspended deposits and withdrawals following at least $75 million in losses during the recent market downturn, echoing 2022 events.

items.title: Trump Family Hosts Mar-a-Lago Crypto Summit with Industry Leaders
items.summary: The Trump family held a crypto summit at Mar-a-Lago attended by Brian Armstrong, David Solomon, and CZ, positioned as a 'show of forces' for World Liberty Financial. Its impact on stalled crypto legislation remains unclear.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Lockheed Martin Flight Tests AI-Enhanced Combat ID on F-35
items.summary: Lockheed Martin successfully demonstrated Project Overwatch, integrating an AI/machine learning model into the F-35's system for real-time combat identification during a flight test at Nellis Air Force Base. The AI resolves emitter ambiguities, improves situational awareness, and can be retrained in minutes for rapid updates.

items.title: Cisco and Sharon AI Launch Australia's First Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA
items.summary: Cisco partnered with Sharon AI to launch Australia's inaugural Cisco Secure AI Factory powered by NVIDIA, delivering secure and high-performance AI infrastructure for enterprises.

items.title: Machine Learning Feature Lineage Tools Market to Reach $1.84 Billion in 2026
items.summary: The global ML feature lineage tools market is projected to grow from $1.51 billion in 2025 to $1.84 billion in 2026 at a 22% CAGR, driven by AI transparency needs, regulatory pressures, and cloud adoption trends.
items.source: EIN Presswire / The Business Research Company

items.title: Hut 8, Tempus AI, and Upstart Highlighted as Top AI Stocks to Watch
items.summary: MarketBeat identifies Hut 8, Tempus AI, and Upstart as key artificial intelligence stocks gaining attention on February 23rd based on their stock screener analysis.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Launches as 2026's Most Anticipated Blockbuster
items.summary: Capcom's Resident Evil Requiem, the latest mainline survival-horror entry, releases this week amid massive hype, high pre-orders, and expectations for psychological tension and next-gen visuals. It's highlighted as the year's first true blockbuster across multiple outlets.

items.title: Nested Lands and City Hunter Release on February 25
items.summary: Nested Lands offers moody exploration with layered environments and survival mechanics, while City Hunter delivers fast-paced urban action combat. Both launch February 25 as part of a varied weekly lineup.

items.title: Tales of Berseria Remastered Arrives February 26
items.summary: The action JRPG remaster enhances visuals and performance while preserving its dark narrative and combo combat, appealing to fans and newcomers alike.

items.title: .hack Franchise Returns After 15 Years for CyberConnect2's 30th Anniversary
items.summary: CyberConnect2 announced a new .hack game to celebrate its 30th anniversary, marking the franchise's revival after over 15 years.

items.title: Bethesda Ports Fallout 4 to Switch 2; Quantic Dream Launches First F2P Game
items.summary: Bethesda is bringing Fallout 4 to Nintendo Switch 2, while Quantic Dream releases its debut free-to-play title, adding to this week's major studio announcements.

items.title: February 2026 Packed with Sequels Including High on Life 2, Nioh 3, and Mario Tennis Fever
items.summary: The month features heavy-hitting sequels like High on Life 2, Nioh 3, and Nintendo's Mario Tennis Fever alongside other AAA and indie releases.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Microsoft Gaming Chief Phil Spencer Leaves After 38 Years
items.summary: Phil Spencer, head of Microsoft gaming, is departing after 38 years, with former Instacart executive Asha Sharma taking over amid challenges for Xbox.

items.title: Apple Plans Early March Product Blitz Across iPhones, MacBooks, and iPads
items.summary: Apple is preparing a multi-day rollout of product announcements in early March, covering updates to iPhones, MacBooks, and iPads as part of its hardware cycle.

items.title: SK Hynix to Expand AI Memory Chip Production for Data Centers
items.summary: SK Hynix plans to increase output of AI memory chips to meet demand from global data-center expansion, highlighting memory as a key bottleneck.

items.title: Meta Secures Multi-Year Deal for Millions of Nvidia Chips
items.summary: Meta has agreed with Nvidia to deploy millions of AI chips, including CPUs and GPUs, for training and inference across its systems.
